name: On-demand full-route data: backend endpoint + athena trigger + status tracking
description:
Today the only files that auto-upload from the device are qlog.zst and qcamera.ts. Full-resolution HEVC (fcamera/ecamera/dcamera.hevc) and full rlog.zst stay on the device unless the route is preserved or the operator pulls them manually. Add an explicit on-demand pull: a single backend call that, for a given route, generates upload URLs for every missing full-res file across every segment, instructs the device to enqueue them via the existing uploadFilesToUrls RPC (internal/ws/methods.go:197), and persists a request row so the UI can poll progress. The backend already has all the pieces -- this feature wires them together end-to-end and adds the status surface.

Dependency note: blocked by route-metadata-from-qlog purely to stagger cmd/server/workers.go writes (this feature, route-metadata-from-qlog, and qcamera-hls-fallback all append a goroutine block there; agent.json forbids force-push, so we serialize their merges instead of forcing a 3-way conflict resolution). There is no logical code dependency on route metadata -- if scheduling allows, the chain can be reordered as long as the three workers.go-touchers merge one after another.
acceptance_criteria: sql/migrations/01N_route_data_requests.up.sql creates a route_data_requests table: id SERIAL PK, route_id INT NOT NULL REFERENCES routes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, requested_by TEXT NOT NULL (ui_users.username; or NULL for jwt-only callers -- pick one and document), requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), kind TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('full_video','full_logs','all')), status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('pending','dispatched','partial','complete','failed')) DEFAULT 'pending', dispatched_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, error TEXT, files_requested INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0. Down migration drops the table

sql/queries/route_data_requests.sql defines: CreateRouteDataRequest, GetRouteDataRequestByID, GetLatestRouteDataRequestByRoute (for the polling UI), UpdateRouteDataRequestStatus, ListPendingRouteDataRequests (for the worker that retries on device reconnect)

internal/api/route_data_request.go (new file) handler with two routes registered in cmd/server/routes.go (NOT main.go): POST /v1/route/:dongle_id/:route_name/request_full_data { kind: 'full_video'|'full_logs'|'all' } -- gated by checkDongleAccess like the other route handlers; GET /v1/route/:dongle_id/:route_name/request_full_data/:request_id returns the row + a derived per-segment progress map computed live from the segments table upload flags

POST handler logic: (1) load segments for the route, (2) for each segment compute the file list per kind -- full_video=[fcamera.hevc, ecamera.hevc, dcamera.hevc], full_logs=[rlog.zst], all=union -- skipping any file already marked uploaded on that segment, (3) for each remaining file generate a server-side upload URL with the same shape and signing as GetUploadURL (refactor internal/api/upload.go to expose a helper that returns the URL+headers given dongle/route/seg/filename so we don't duplicate the construction), (4) build a slice of UploadFileToUrlParams with fn=<route_name>--<seg>/<filename> matching openpilot's athenad layout (cross-check against ../../openpilot/system/athena/athenad.py), (5) call CallUploadFilesToUrls via d.rpcCaller against the route's dongle WS client, (6) persist the request row with files_requested = len(items), set status=dispatched on success or failed (with error) on RPC error

If the device is not currently connected on the WS hub, the handler still creates the row with status=pending and returns 202 Accepted. A small reconciliation worker (internal/worker/route_data_request_dispatcher.go) wakes every PollInterval (default 30s) and retries dispatch for pending rows whose route's dongle is now online -- bounded retry count, then status=failed with a clear error. Wire behind ROUTE_DATA_DISPATCHER_ENABLED=true in cmd/server/workers.go

Idempotency: if a non-failed request for the same (route_id, kind) exists newer than 1h, the POST returns the existing row with 200 OK rather than dispatching a duplicate batch. This stops accidental double-uploads when a user double-clicks the UI button

Status derivation: the GET endpoint reads the request row plus the segments table's upload flags and returns { request: {...}, progress: { files_requested, files_uploaded, percent }, segments: [{ segment_number, fcamera_uploaded, ecamera_uploaded, dcamera_uploaded, rlog_uploaded } ...] } so the UI doesn't need to hit two endpoints

Status auto-completes: if files_uploaded == files_requested at GET time, the handler updates the row to status=complete and stamps completed_at before returning. (Cheap to do on read; avoids needing yet another worker)
Auth: same checkDongleAccess used by the existing route handlers (session-or-JWT). Do not add a new auth surface

Tests: internal/api/route_data_request_test.go covers happy path (queues N items via stubbed RPC caller), kind filtering (full_logs requests only rlog.zst), idempotent re-request within 1h, device-offline fallback (row stays pending, no RPC call), and the GET status endpoint's auto-complete behavior. internal/worker/route_data_request_dispatcher_test.go covers the retry-on-reconnect and bounded-retry-then-fail paths

Manual verification on the deployed backend: against the existing route 92fc6a8b6c034662/0000004a--ce3ac77767 (12 segments, only qlog.zst + qcamera.ts uploaded), POST request_full_data with kind=all and confirm a route_data_requests row is created and the device receives an uploadFilesToUrls RPC over the WS
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
